summary: Tested Bulk Category Editor scope attributes save mechanism and Force Save button. All key features VERIFIED: (1) Force Save button present with data-testid, (2) Standard save path works with id_field parameter, (3) Save status banner shows success/failure counts, (4) 'Currently Saved' tags refresh after save, (5) Saved attributes persist when reopening modal. Backend API tests: 8/9 passed (1 minor failure with fields_to_clear format).

minor.issue: fields_to_clear parameter expects array values for $in operator, but test sent boolean. Returns 500 error: '$in needs an array'. This is a minor edge case - the main save functionality works correctly.

critical_code_review_comments: Force Save button (lines 11218-11236) correctly uses data-testid='force-save-btn' and amber styling
Save status banner (lines 11133-11169) correctly shows success/failure counts with data-testid='save-status-banner'
Force Save progress indicator (lines 11171-11196) correctly shows progress with data-testid='force-save-progress'
handleForceSave function (lines 4048-4220) correctly sends each product individually using getProductIdForApi()
Standard save path (lines 3967-4030) correctly groups products by id_field (sku vs supplier_code)
refreshBreakdownsAfterSave function (lines 4305-4312) correctly recomputes breakdowns from fresh product data
computeFieldBreakdowns function (lines 4246-4302) correctly computes value breakdowns for 'Currently Saved' tags

success_rate.backend: 89% - 8/9 tests passed (1 minor fields_to_clear format issue)
success_rate.frontend: 100% - All UI elements and flows verified

seed_data_creation: None - used existing Canopy supplier products
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bulk Category Editor scope save mechanism VERIFIED: (1) Force Save button (amber) sends each product individually for maximum reliability, (2) Standard save groups products by id_field (sku vs supplier_code), (3) Save status banner appears after save showing success count, (4) 'Currently Saved' tags update immediately after save via refreshBreakdownsAfterSave(), (5) Force Save progress bar shows during save. Test product used: Blenheim Oak from Canopy supplier.

1_force_save_button_present.details: Force Save button found with data-testid='force-save-btn' and amber/orange styling

2_apply_button_present.details: Apply to N Products button found with purple styling

3_cancel_button_present.details: Cancel button found in dialog footer

4_force_save_functionality.details: Force Save clicked successfully, progress indicator shown, toast notification 'Force Save complete: 1/1 products saved successfully'

5_save_status_banner.details: Green save status banner appeared showing 'All 1 products saved successfully' with timestamp

6_currently_saved_tags_refresh.details: 'Currently Saved' section shows 'Wall Tiles (1)' and 'Kitchen Tiles (1)' with 'Mixed' label after save

7_persistence_after_reopen.details: After closing and reopening modal, 'Currently Saved' still shows the saved categories

10_backend_multiple_products.details: Bulk update with multiple product IDs works correctly

12_backend_fields_to_clear.details: fields_to_clear with boolean value returns 500 error - expects array for $in operator
